## [00:00 - 03:04]

Hey everybody, it is Dami here, the marketing manager at Smart Marketer. I wanted to walk you guys through how I go through and create content and then distribute it across our social channels for Smart Marketer. So as some of you may know, we release a podcast two times a week and it's everything from business to lifestyle. So I really like to do a mixture of content. So what I do is I go through and I'm a super visual person. So I like to go through and take notes on the podcast, the main takeaways, and then I can also use this content for a later date if I'm like, hey, I, you know, want to give some people some more business tips because for us in looking at our analytics, I know that tips about anything business related works really well for us. So I have a content bank to pull from. So before I decide which bullet to choose, I have an idea in mind of what type of graphic I want to create. You know, is it a video? Is it going to be a carousel post? And so for this, I knew that I wanted to boost it on Instagram and you are not allowed to boost carousel posts on Instagram. So for this post, I wanted something that was going to be a tip that people could see coming from John. That was going to be a single image. So after looking through, listening to the podcast, I decided that morning pages was going to be my main focus of this piece of content. And basically what this is, is it's going to be a post about how John utilizes this technique and it is called morning pages. You write for 20 to 30 minutes, you can write anything down. You want to write about three pages before you start your day. So what I'll do is I'll go into Canva and I will create a graphic that I think would look good with this post. So as you guys can see, here's the post that I created. So I just put in a picture of John. I know he wasn't super stoked about his hair in this picture, but we used it anyways. And I thought that a quote would look good on this because I think that this is not a common enough method for people to just say, oh yeah, I put morning pages on here. Someone would understand what that meant. So I put a quote from John and then in here, in our description, oops sorry, in our caption, I broke down a little bit more about what

## [03:05 - 05:57]

this was and put some more context around it. So after I have everything posted, and you guys can see me do this in live time because I haven't posted this yet or I haven't boosted this yet. So we have a campaign for our smart marketer account. And the campaign objective that you want to choose is post engagement. So that means people are going to be engaging with our posts. I'm doing some testing of writing these to cold audiences, but typically we only run these to our warm audiences and by warm audience, I mean this is anybody who has engaged with us in the past. So anybody who's watched Molly's stuff, Ezra's stuff, any of our customer lists. And then I put in all the countries that I want. And then I will go into the actual ad section here. And then I will look for an old ad. And then I typically just duplicate them. As you can see, we have tons of ads because I'm in here doing this probably, I mean every time we have a post, we boost it. We do about $50 a day. But for the smart marketer account, so I will duplicate it. Okay. And then I will name it something. That makes sense. John productivity. Okay. Facebook page. Smart marketer. And then I will select a post. And this is the post that I want. And then I will push publish. And then once that's published, that is ready to go and you have that boosted on Instagram.
